skill.name: azure-storage-blob-rust
skill.description: Guidance for using the Azure Blob Storage SDK in Rust to authenticate, create clients, and manage blobs and containers.
skill.summary: Build Rust applications that use Azure Blob Storage clients, authentication, blob operations, and container operations.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static command-execution findings are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline Rust identifiers, and one normal installation command. Hardcoded URLs are expected Azure service, documentation, source, and package links; the weak cryptography finding at SKILL.md line 3 has no supporting evidence.

low_findings.title: Static command execution findings are Markdown examples
low_findings.description: The detected backtick patterns are Markdown code fences and inline Rust identifiers. The only shell command shown is a normal cargo dependency installation command, and the skill does not execute commands itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are documentation snippets, not executable skill code. No evidence found that the skill runs shell commands or builds commands from user input.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs are expected Azure and package references
low_findings.description: The URLs point to Azure Blob service endpoints, docs.rs, GitHub source, and crates.io. They support legitimate SDK examples and references, with no evidence of credential exfiltration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The domains and endpoint format match the stated Azure Blob Storage purpose. No suspicious third-party collection endpoint or secret-bearing request is present.

low_findings.title: Weak cryptography finding is unsupported by context
low_findings.description: The static high-risk weak cryptography hit points to the front matter description line. No weak algorithm, hash call, or cryptographic implementation appears at that location.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 starts a YAML description block and contains no cryptographic algorithm. I did not find evidence supporting the weak cryptography alert.

low_findings.title: Examples include write and delete operations against cloud storage
low_findings.description: The skill documents upload, create container, and delete blob operations. These are expected features, but users should test with least-privilege Azure roles before applying them to production data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The operational behavior is clear from the documented SDK calls. The concern is limited because the skill is instructional and does not perform the operations directly.

content.user_title: Build Azure Blob Storage Rust Workflows
content.value_statement: Rust developers need clear SDK patterns for Azure Blob Storage. This skill provides focused guidance for authentication, clients, blob operations, container operations, and RBAC planning.
actual_capabilities: Shows how to add Azure Blob Storage and identity crates to a Rust project.
Explains DeveloperToolsCredential and ManagedIdentityCredential usage for Azure authentication.
Demonstrates BlobClient creation with an Azure Blob service endpoint, container name, and blob name.
Provides examples for uploading, downloading, reading properties, and deleting blobs.
Covers BlobContainerClient usage for creating containers and listing blobs.
Lists Azure RBAC roles needed for read, write, and ownership scenarios.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not validate Azure account settings.
It does not include a complete runnable Rust project or tests.
It does not cover every Azure Storage feature, such as queues, tables, lifecycle rules, or SAS generation.
It assumes the user supplies secure Azure credentials and correct RBAC assignments.

use_cases.title: Add Blob Uploads to a Rust Service
use_cases.description: Use the SDK examples to create a BlobClient, wrap upload content, and send files to an Azure container.

use_cases.title: Review Azure Storage Authentication
use_cases.description: Compare development and production credential patterns before deploying Rust services to Azure.

use_cases.title: Prototype Container Browsing
use_cases.description: Use the container client guidance to list blobs and inspect container contents during a proof of concept.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Blob Client
prompt_templates.prompt: Using the azure-storage-blob-rust skill, show me the steps to create a Rust BlobClient for my Azure storage account.

prompt_templates.title: Implement Blob Upload and Download
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the Azure Blob Storage Rust guidance to outline upload and download logic for a service that stores user files.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Production Authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how I should adapt the Rust Azure Blob examples for production authentication with managed identity and least-privilege RBAC.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Storage Access Layer
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design a Rust storage module that separates BlobClient creation, upload, download, delete, and container listing responsibilities.

output_examples.input: Explain how to upload a blob from a Rust service.
output_examples.output: A concise implementation plan covering crate installation, credential selection, BlobClient creation, request content preparation, content length, and overwrite behavior.

output_examples.input: Help me decide which Azure role my service needs.
output: Storage Blob Data Reader for read-only downloads and listing.
Storage Blob Data Contributor for upload, update, and delete workflows.
Storage Blob Data Owner only when the service must manage ownership or RBAC.

output_examples.input: Review my Azure Blob Rust design before deployment.
output_examples.output: A checklist covering managed identity, account endpoint configuration, async runtime use, content length handling, delete safeguards, and production RBAC.

best_practices: Use Entra ID and managed identity for production services instead of long-lived secrets.
Apply least-privilege Azure RBAC roles for each service and environment.
Test upload, download, list, and delete behavior against non-production containers first.
anti_patterns: Do not use owner-level permissions when reader or contributor permissions are enough.
Do not run delete examples against production containers without retention and recovery controls.
Do not hardcode real storage account names, credentials, or container names in source code.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps build Rust code that connects to Azure Blob Storage and performs common blob and container operations.

faq.question: Does the skill run Azure commands for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and examples. The user decides what code or commands to run.

faq.question: Which authentication method should I use in production?
faq.answer: Use managed identity with Entra ID when possible. Assign only the Azure RBAC role required by the service.

faq.question: Can it help with uploads and downloads?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for upload content, content length, download responses, and blob properties.

faq.question: Does it cover container operations?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes examples for creating containers and listing blobs with BlobContainerClient.

faq.question: Is this a full Azure Storage tutorial?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on Azure Blob Storage SDK patterns for Rust, not all Azure Storage services.
